texto: She had never been able to stand her husband, though not for one minute in their married life had she permitted this to make her unhappy. Only people who are fond of somebody can ever be unhappy, she had told her daughter before her wedding.
explicacao: Citação de Anna Seghers sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “She had never been able to stand her husband, though not for one minute in their married life had…” articula felicidade, happiness, love em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como casamento e love.

seo.titleSeo: "She had never been able to stand her husband, though…" — Anna Seghers | Metamensagem

frase_original: She had never been able to stand her husband, though not for one minute in their married life had she permitted this to make her unhappy. Only people who are fond of somebody can ever be unhappy, she had told her daughter before her wedding.

texto: What magic was this, brewed from equal parts of age-old memories and total oblivion. One could have believed that the last war these people had fought had left only happy memories, had carried in its wake nothing but joy and prosperity. Women and girls were smiling as if their sons and lovers were invulnerable.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anna Seghers: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “What magic was this, brewed from equal parts of age-old memories and total oblivion. One could ha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, hubris, memory pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de memory e war.
